desc: Expressões do dia a dia de grandes corporações e consultorias.

cards.def: To use something strategically to achieve maximum advantage from existing resources or capabilities.
cards.ex: "We need to leverage our existing client relationships to accelerate market entry into the Brazilian Northeast."
cards.tip: Top 5 verbs in consulting. "Leverage capabilities", "leverage synergies". Aprenda a usá-lo naturalmente em pitch.

cards.def: The state of agreement and shared direction among stakeholders toward common strategic objectives.
cards.ex: "Before we proceed, we need full alignment across the leadership team on the go-forward strategy."
cards.tip: Mais estratégico que "agreement". "Get alignment" = processo ativo. Crítico em client engagements.

cards.def: Informal: the available time or capacity a person or team has to take on additional responsibilities.
cards.ex: "I don't have the bandwidth to lead the Asian market expansion given current project commitments."
cards.tip: Metáfora de telecom usada figurativamente. Muito comum em consulting e tech. Aceito em C-suite.

cards.def: A thorough, detailed examination of a specific topic going beyond surface-level insights.
cards.ex: "Let's schedule a deep dive into the supply chain data before we finalize the client presentation."
cards.tip: Muito usado na McKinsey. Implica ir além da análise de primeiro nível — busca root causes e insights não óbvios.

cards.def: To return to a topic at a later time, typically after gathering more information.
cards.ex: "I don't have the numbers right now — let me circle back with you by end of day with the full breakdown."
cards.tip: Equivale a "follow up" com nuance de revisitar algo pendente. Extremamente comum em reuniões corporativas.

cards.def: Specific tasks assigned to named individuals as concrete outcomes of a meeting, with clear owners and deadlines.
cards.ex: "Let's close with action items: Sarah owns the client deck, James drives vendor outreach — both due Friday."
cards.tip: Essencial em meeting facilitation. Bom PM/consultor fecha reuniões com action items: owner + deadline + output.

cards.def: A statement of the unique benefits and differentiated value a solution delivers to its intended audience.
cards.ex: "Our value proposition centers on speed-to-market combined with a 40% cost reduction vs. the incumbent."
cards.tip: Fundamental em strategy consulting. Deve responder: Why us? Why now? For whom? At what cost?

cards.def: Easily achievable improvements that require minimal effort relative to the value they deliver.
cards.ex: "Before the full restructuring, let's identify the low-hanging fruit — quick wins to build momentum."
cards.tip: Similar a "quick win" mas enfatiza facilidade. Cuidado: overuse pode soar clichê em audiences sofisticadas.

cards.def: Specific, recurring problems experienced by clients that a solution aims to address.
cards.ex: "Discovery interviews revealed that compliance reporting is the client's primary pain point, consuming 30% of analyst time."
cards.tip: Central em problem-solving, product development e sales. Identificar pain points precede qualquer solution design.

cards.def: The active support, agreement, and commitment obtained from key stakeholders for a plan or change initiative.
cards.ex: "Securing executive buy-in early eliminated the political obstacles that derailed last year's transformation."
cards.tip: "Get buy-in" = processo de convencer. "Secure buy-in" = obter comprometimento formal. Tema central em org change.

cards.def: A strategic plan specifying how an organization will deliver its product to target customers.
cards.ex: "Our go-to-market strategy prioritizes enterprise accounts in three LATAM metros before broader expansion."
cards.tip: GTM para abreviar. Inclui: target segment, channels, pricing, messaging. Frequente em strategy decks.

cards.def: A method that has consistently produced superior results and is recognized as the standard to follow.
cards.ex: "Industry best practices suggest a structured 30/60/90-day integration roadmap for acquisitions at this scale."
cards.tip: Plural: "best practices". Consultores usam para validar recommendations. Peer benchmarking = finding best practices.

cards.desc: Expressões, verbos e substantivos essenciais para o dia a dia em consultorias e multinacionais. Selecionados para comunicação real em reuniões, e-mails e apresentações.

cards.def: To make contact or have a brief conversation with someone to update them or get their input.
cards.ex: "Let's touch base next week to see how the client presentation is coming along."
cards.tip: Muito usado em e-mails e conversas informais. Não é um verbo formal, mas extremamente comum.

cards.def: To become informed about the latest developments or status of a project or situation.
cards.ex: "I just joined the team, can you get me up to speed on the client's main concerns?"
cards.tip: Perfeito para quando você entra em um projeto novo. Mostra proatividade.

cards.def: To contact someone, especially to ask for help or to offer something.
cards.ex: "I'll reach out to the IT department to get the access credentials."
cards.tip: Substituto moderno para 'contact'. Soa mais acessível e colaborativo.

cards.def: To ensure someone is informed about progress and developments.
cards.ex: "Please keep the regional director in the loop as we negotiate the contract."
cards.tip: Essencial em gestão de stakeholders. Evita surpresas desagradáveis.

cards.def: To start an activity or process, often to avoid delay.
cards.ex: "We have the green light, so let's get the ball rolling on the market analysis."
cards.tip: Expressão idiomática que transmite senso de urgência e ação.

cards.pt: adiar / deixar para depois (US) | colocar em pauta (UK)
cards.def: US: to postpone discussion of a topic. UK: to bring a topic forward for discussion.
cards.ex: US: "Let's table that idea until we have more data." UK: "We need to table the budget proposal at the next meeting."
cards.tip: Cuidado: significado oposto nos EUA e Reino Unido. No Brasil, use o sentido americano (adiar) ou especifique.

cards.def: To mark something as important or needing attention, often a problem or risk.
cards.ex: "I'd like to flag a potential delay in the supplier delivery."
cards.tip: Comum em e-mails e reuniões para levantar preocupações sem soar alarmista.

cards.def: To examine or investigate something in greater detail.
cards.ex: "The EBITDA margin is below target; we need to drill down into the cost components."
cards.tip: Sinônimo de 'deep dive'. Muito usado em análise de dados e relatórios.

cards.def: A detailed plan for achieving success, especially in a competitive situation.
cards.ex: "What's the game plan for the client meeting tomorrow? Who's presenting what?"
cards.tip: Mais informal que 'strategy'. Passa a ideia de tática prática.

cards.def: To show or explain something to someone to get their opinion or approval.
cards.ex: "I have a draft of the proposal; let me run it by you before I send it to the client."
cards.tip: Educado e colaborativo. Mostra que você valoriza a opinião do outro.

cards.def: To be in agreement or have a shared understanding about something.
cards.ex: "Before we talk to the client, let's make sure we're on the same page about the pricing."
cards.tip: Clássico. Use para evitar mal-entendidos.

cards.def: Someone who argues against the popular opinion to test the strength of an idea.
cards.ex: "Let me play devil's advocate here – what if the competitor drops their price by 20%?"
cards.tip: Valioso em sessões de brainstorming. Mostra pensamento crítico.

cards.def: To reply or get back to someone with information or a decision.
cards.ex: "I'll check with the legal team and revert by end of day."
cards.tip: Muito usado em e-mails em ambientes corporativos, especialmente na Europa e Ásia. Nos EUA, preferem 'get back to you'.

cards.def: To send a quick message (email, chat, SMS) to someone, usually to remind or follow up.
cards.ex: "I'll ping you on Slack when the numbers are ready."
cards.tip: Informal, comum em ambientes tech e startups. Deriva do comando de rede.

cards.pt: tentar fazer algo impossível / querer demais
cards.def: To attempt something that is too ambitious or impossible, wasting time and resources.
cards.ex: "We don't need to boil the ocean – let's focus on the top three markets first."
cards.tip: Expressão forte para alinhar expectativas e evitar escopo excessivo.

cards.pt: estado da arte / tecnologia de ponta (com risco)
cards.def: Technology or solutions that are so advanced they are unproven and risky.
cards.ex: "The client wants to use bleeding-edge AI, but we need to consider the reliability risks."
cards.tip: Diferente de 'cutting edge' (que é avançado mas testado). Este é mais arriscado.

cards.def: Easy opportunities or quick wins that require minimal effort.
cards.ex: "Let's go after the low-hanging fruit first – we can implement those changes in two weeks."
cards.tip: Já existente (c08), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: A project or action that delivers immediate results with minimal effort.
cards.ex: "Reducing meeting frequency is a quick win that will free up team time."
cards.tip: Ideal para construir momentum em projetos de transformação.

cards.def: An issue that prevents progress on a task or project.
cards.ex: "The only blocker right now is the client's approval on the budget."
cards.tip: Comum em metodologias ágeis (Scrum). Use em daily stand-ups.

cards.def: A place to note topics that are not relevant to the current discussion but should be addressed later.
cards.ex: "That's an important point, but let's put it in the parking lot and come back to it."
cards.tip: Técnica de facilitação de reuniões para manter o foco.

cards.pt: ação prática / que pode ser executado
cards.def: Something that can be acted upon or implemented.
cards.ex: "The report needs to provide actionable insights, not just raw data."
cards.tip: Diferente de 'theoretical'. Resultado deve ser prático.

cards.def: A tangible or intangible output of a project phase (report, prototype, software).
cards.ex: "The main deliverable for this sprint is the user authentication module."
cards.tip: Já existente (p04), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: A significant event or achievement in a project, usually with zero duration.
cards.ex: "The project's next milestone is the prototype sign-off on June 15th."
cards.tip: Já existente (p09), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: The amount of time or mental capacity a person has for additional work.
cards.ex: "I'd love to help, but I don't have the bandwidth this week."
cards.tip: Já existente (c03), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: Alternative to 'circle back', common in some regions.
cards.ex: "I'll circular back with you once I have the figures."
cards.tip: Variação de 'circle back' (c05). Ambos são usados.

cards.def: To discuss a topic outside the current meeting, often because it's too detailed or off-topic.
cards.ex: "This is a complex issue; let's take it offline and continue with the agenda."
cards.tip: Educado e eficiente para gerenciar tempo em reuniões.

cards.def: To focus on or explore a topic in more detail (metaphor from computing).
cards.ex: "I'd like to double-click on the marketing numbers – why did we see a drop in Q3?"
cards.tip: Muito usado em apresentações e reuniões de consultoria.

cards.def: A quick test to ensure an idea, plan, or number is reasonable and not obviously wrong.
cards.ex: "Let's do a sanity check on these projections before presenting to the board."
cards.tip: Diferente de 'validação completa'. É uma verificação rápida de senso comum.

cards.def: To reassess and adjust plans, expectations, or strategies based on new information.
cards.ex: "Given the new competitor launch, we need to recalibrate our pricing strategy."
cards.tip: Mais sofisticado que 'change'. Implica um ajuste fino baseado em análise.

cards.def: To be the main or most important reason for something.
cards.ex: "All these issues boil down to a lack of communication between teams."
cards.tip: Útil para sintetizar problemas complexos.

cards.def: To be noticed or considered as important or of interest.
cards.ex: "The potential merger has been on our radar for months."
cards.tip: Comum para falar de oportunidades ou ameaças futuras.

cards.def: A rough calculation or estimate, done quickly without detailed analysis.
cards.ex: "A back-of-the-envelope calculation suggests we'll need at least $2M."
cards.tip: Mostra capacidade de estimativa rápida, comum em consultoria.

cards.def: The sequence of stages determining the minimum time needed for an operation.
cards.ex: "The design approval is on the critical path – any delay pushes the entire launch."
cards.tip: Já existente (p02), mantido pela relevância.

cards.pt: abrir o capô / examinar por dentro
cards.def: To look closely at how something works internally, especially to find problems.
cards.ex: "The numbers look good, but let's lift the hood and see the assumptions behind them."
cards.tip: Metáfora automotiva. Mostra ceticismo saudável e rigor analítico.

cards.def: A department or group that does not share information or collaborate with others.
cards.ex: "Working in silos led to duplicated efforts and conflicting messages to the client."
cards.tip: Crítico em discussões sobre cultura organizacional e eficiência.

cards.def: To perform calculations or financial analysis to evaluate a situation.
cards.ex: "We need to run the numbers before committing to that acquisition."
cards.tip: Muito comum em finanças e estratégia.

cards.def: Brainstorming without constraints, focusing on innovative ideas rather than practical limitations.
cards.ex: "Let's do some blue-sky thinking – if budget were no object, what would we propose?"
cards.tip: Bom para sessões de inovação. Diferente de pensamento estratégico pragmático.

cards.def: Synonym for 'drill down' or 'deep dive'.
cards.ex: "We need to drill into the customer churn data to understand the root cause."
cards.tip: Intercambiável com 'drill down'.

cards.def: To challenge or resist an idea, plan, or decision.
cards.ex: "The client pushed back on the proposed timeline, saying it was too aggressive."
cards.tip: Habilidade importante: saber quando e como 'push back' construtivamente.

cards.def: To add someone to an email thread or conversation to keep them informed.
cards.ex: "I'll loop in the legal team so they're aware of the contract changes."
cards.tip: Correlato de 'keep in the loop'.

cards.def: To make a significant change or difference in a situation or metric.
cards.ex: "Small efficiency gains won't move the needle; we need a radical redesign."
cards.tip: Usado para separar ações marginais de ações transformadoras.

cards.def: A metaphor for a process that happens so gradually that people don't notice the negative impact until it's too late.
cards.ex: "Increasing prices slowly over years is like boiling the frog – customers don't notice until it's too high."
cards.tip: Menos comum que 'boil the ocean', mas usado em contextos de gestão de mudança.

cards.def: A traditional project management approach where phases are sequential and distinct.
cards.ex: "The client prefers a waterfall methodology with clear milestones and sign-offs."
cards.tip: Contraponto a 'agile'. Útil em discussões de metodologia.

cards.def: An iterative approach to project management, often used in software, emphasizing flexibility and collaboration.
cards.ex: "We'll use an agile approach with two-week sprints to adapt to changing requirements."
cards.tip: Mais que uma metodologia, uma mentalidade. Use com confiança.

cards.def: A specific agile framework for managing complex projects, with roles like Scrum Master and Product Owner.
cards.ex: "Our team runs scrum with daily stand-ups and sprint reviews."
cards.tip: Conhecimento básico em ambientes de tecnologia e transformação digital.

cards.def: A methodology focused on minimizing waste and maximizing value, originating from manufacturing.
cards.ex: "We applied lean principles to streamline the approval process, cutting wait times by 40%."
cards.tip: Aplicável a qualquer processo, não só manufatura.

cards.def: The final profit or loss after all expenses; also used figuratively to mean the most important point.
cards.ex: "The bottom line is that we need to increase revenue or cut costs."
cards.tip: Clássico. Use literal (finanças) ou figurado (conclusão principal).

cards.def: Gross revenue or sales, before any costs are subtracted.
cards.ex: "The new product boosted our top line by 15%, but margins remain thin."
cards.tip: Sempre contrastado com 'bottom line'.

cards.def: A projection of future financial performance based on current data, often annualizing monthly figures.
cards.ex: "Based on Q1 results, our run rate for the year is $12M."
cards.tip: Útil para startups e negócios sazonais.

cards.def: Being profitable or having positive earnings.
cards.ex: "After three years of losses, the company is finally in the black."
cards.tip: Antônimo: 'in the red' (no vermelho).

cards.def: Being unprofitable or having negative earnings.
cards.ex: "The division has been in the red for two consecutive quarters."
cards.tip: Origem: tinta vermelha usada para registrar perdas.

cards.def: Operating expenditures – day-to-day costs of running a business (rent, salaries, utilities).
cards.ex: "Moving to the cloud reduced our CapEx but increased OpEx."
cards.tip: Já existente (f06), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: Capital expenditures – funds used to acquire or upgrade long-term assets (equipment, buildings).
cards.ex: "The board approved $5M in CapEx for the new factory."
cards.tip: Já existente (f05), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: Return on investment – a measure of profitability relative to cost.
cards.ex: "The marketing campaign delivered an ROI of 300%."
cards.tip: Já existente (f04), mantido pela relevância.

cards.def: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization – a proxy for operating cash flow.
cards.ex: "The acquisition target has a healthy EBITDA margin of 25%."
cards.tip: Já existente (f01), mantido pela relevância.

cards.pt: gerir com disciplina / com mão firme
cards.def: To manage an organization or department efficiently and strictly.
cards.ex: "The new COO runs a tight ship – meetings start on time and budgets are strictly followed."
cards.tip: Expressão idiomática para liderança disciplinada.

cards.pt: detalhar / ir ao nível de detalhe
cards.def: To examine or discuss something in very fine detail.
cards.ex: "We need to get granular on the cost drivers – line by line."
cards.tip: Sinônimo de 'drill down' mas com ênfase em detalhe extremo.

cards.def: To phase out or discontinue a product, service, or system over time.
cards.ex: "The legacy software will be sunsetted over the next 12 months."
cards.tip: Usado como verbo. Mais suave que 'kill' ou 'cancel'.

cards.def: A project that lacks constraints imposed by prior work, starting from scratch.
cards.ex: "We have a greenfield opportunity to design the system without legacy integration issues."
cards.tip: Opõe-se a 'brownfield' (projeto com legado).

cards.def: A project that must consider or integrate with existing systems and constraints.
cards.ex: "The brownfield nature of the project makes it more complex than building from scratch."
cards.tip: Comum em TI e engenharia.

cards.def: A strategy of moving a system or process to a new environment without making changes.
cards.ex: "The first phase is a lift and shift of the data center to the cloud."
cards.tip: Comum em migrações de TI. Fase inicial antes de otimizações.

cards.def: A metaphor for a process that happens so gradually that people don't notice the negative impact until it's too late.
cards.ex: "Increasing prices slowly over years is like boiling the frog – customers don't notice until it's too high."
cards.tip: Usado em contextos de gestão de mudança e riscos.

cards.def: To perform calculations or financial analysis to evaluate a situation.
cards.ex: "We need to run the numbers before committing to that acquisition."
cards.tip: Muito comum em finanças e estratégia.

cards.def: A quick test to ensure an idea, plan, or number is reasonable and not obviously wrong.
cards.ex: "Let's do a sanity check on these projections before presenting to the board."
cards.tip: Diferente de 'validação completa'. É uma verificação rápida de senso comum.

cards.def: A first draft or preliminary version of a proposal, meant to be critiqued and improved.
cards.ex: "Here's a straw man of the org chart – feel free to tear it apart."
cards.tip: Comum em consultoria para iniciar discussões. Não é pejorativo neste contexto.

cards.def: A slide presentation, usually in PowerPoint or Keynote.
cards.ex: "I'll send you the deck after the meeting so you can review the details."
cards.tip: Onipresente em consultoria. 'Pitch deck', 'board deck'.

cards.def: To put maximum effort into something, leaving nothing in reserve.
cards.ex: "We left it all on the field for this pitch – I'm proud of the team regardless of the outcome."
cards.tip: Metáfora esportiva. Usada para motivar equipes.

cards.def: To pay close attention to small details, often to ensure quality.
cards.ex: "He's a great project manager because he sweats the details without losing sight of the big picture."
cards.tip: Qualidade valorizada em consultoria: rigor analítico.

cards.def: Support from senior leaders that protects a team or project from interference or criticism.
cards.ex: "With the CEO's air cover, we could push through the restructuring despite resistance."
cards.tip: Metáfora militar. Essencial em projetos políticos.

cards.pt: equipe de elite para resolver problemas
cards.def: A specialized, cross-functional team assembled to solve a specific, often urgent, problem.
cards.ex: "We formed a tiger team to address the cybersecurity breach."
cards.tip: Origem militar/NASA. Usado para crises ou desafios críticos.
